title: Newly Discovered PowMix Botnet Hits Czech Workers Using Randomized C2 Traffic
summary: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of an active malicious campaign that's targeting the workforce in the Czech Republic with a previously undocumented botnet dubbed PowMix since at least December 2025.
email_summary: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of an active malicious campaign that's targeting the workforce in the Czech Republic with a previously undocumented botnet dubbed PowMix since at least December 2025.
card_summary: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of an active malicious campaign that's targeting the workforce in the Czech Republic with a previously undocumented botnet dubbed PowMix since at least December 2025.

title: NIST Revamps CVE Framework to Focus on High-Impact Vulnerabilities
summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology carved a new path for vulnerability remediation by changing the way it prioritizes software flaws.
email_summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology carved a new path for vulnerability remediation by changing the way it prioritizes software flaws.
card_summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology carved a new path for vulnerability remediation by changing the way it prioritizes software flaws.

title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
summary: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.
email_summary: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.
card_summary: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs.

title: 'Harmless' Global Adware Transforms Into an AV Killer
summary: A benign looking update Dragon Boss pushed out in March 2025 established persistence via scheduled tasks and arranged for future payloads to be excluded from Windows Defender.
email_summary: A benign looking update Dragon Boss pushed out in March 2025 established persistence via scheduled tasks and arranged for future payloads to be excluded from Windows Defender.
card_summary: A benign looking update Dragon Boss pushed out in March 2025 established persistence via scheduled tasks and arranged for future payloads to be excluded from Windows Defender.

title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Defender 0-Day, SonicWall Brute-Force, 17-Year-Old Excel RCE and 15 More Stories
summary: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? Yeah. This week delivered. We've got hackers getting creative in ways that are almost impressive if you ignore the whole "crime" part, ancient vulnerabilities somehow still ruining people's days, and enough supply chain drama to fill a season of television nobody asked for.

sections.context: This story is part of the recurring zero-day pattern where exploitation pressure is highest before broad patch adoption.

email_summary: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot. Severity is rated critical.
card_summary: You know that feeling when you open your feed on a Thursday morning and it's just... a lot? This week delivered.

title: Operation PowerOFF identifies 75k DDoS users, takes down 53 domains

title: ZionSiphon malware designed to sabotage water treatment systems
summary: A new malware called ZionSiphon, specifically designed for operational technology, is targeting water treatment and desalination environments to sabotage their operations. [...]

title: [Webinar] Find and Eliminate Orphaned Non-Human Identities in Your Environment
summary: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching.

sections.context:
This reflects continued use of phishing as an initial-access path in broader intrusion chains.

This fits the broader data-exposure trend where follow-on abuse of leaked information is common.

email_summary: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not weak passwords.
card_summary: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not weak passwords.

title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
summary: Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service.
email_summary: Cisco has announced patches to address four critical security flaws impacting Identity Services and Webex Services that could result in arbitrary code execution and allow an attacker to impersonate any user within the service. Severity is rated critical.

title: New Microsoft Defender “RedSun” zero-day PoC grants SYSTEM privileges
summary: A researcher known as "Chaotic Eclipse" has published a proof-of-concept exploit for a second Microsoft Defender zero-day, dubbed "RedSun," in the past two weeks, protesting how the company works with cybersecurity researchers. [...]

sections.context: This story is part of the recurring zero-day pattern where exploitation pressure is highest before broad patch adoption.

title: Hidden Passenger? How Taboola Routes Logged-In Banking Sessions to Temu
summary: A bank approved a Taboola pixel. That pixel quietly redirected logged-in users to a Temu tracking endpoint. This occurred without the bank’s knowledge, without user consent, and without a single security control registering a violation.
email_summary: A bank approved a Taboola pixel. That pixel quietly redirected logged-in users to a Temu tracking endpoint.
card_summary: A bank approved a Taboola pixel. That pixel quietly redirected logged-in users to a Temu tracking endpoint.

title: Obsidian Plugin Abuse Delivers PHANTOMPULSE RAT in Targeted Finance, Crypto Attacks
summary: A "novel" social engineering campaign has been observed abusing Obsidian, a cross-platform note-taking application, as an initial access vector to distribute a previously undocumented Windows remote access trojan called PHANTOMPULSE in attacks targeting individuals in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors.

sections.overview: A "novel" social engineering campaign has been observed abusing Obsidian, a cross-platform note-taking application, as an initial access vector to distribute a previously undocumented Windows remote access trojan called PHANTOMPULSE in attacks targeting individuals in the financial and cryptocurrency sectors.
